content: I am trying to implement a simple Linux kernel module that read from/write to a vector through miscdev in Rust. I am trying to implement the seek() method in file::Operations for my rust module. However, I am confused about how I could modify the file offset. I could get the current cursor in fd using &File but since it's immutable, I cannot change the cursor based on the SeekFrom passed in. How should I implement seek appropriately in this case? Thanks.

Ariel Miculas said:

just return the new offset? https://www.makelinux.net/ldd3/chp-6-sect-5.shtml

It seems to me that the file operations in Rust haven't ported the full functionality in C so the offset is not really used. I ended up keeping an offset inside the memory module in Rust.
Is it not enough to return the new offset? What exactly are you trying to achieve?
